meta.description: Non-Latvian boxing venues used for Baltic, European, World, and Olympic events. Capacities are real-world figures verified against public records. Latvian venues live in nations/latvia/boxing/venues.json. All venue files share a global id namespace — ids must be unique across the set. Venues are assigned to events at generation time by the engine picking from the eligible pool in event-templates.json. Adding a new venue requires only adding an entry to the appropriate file — no engine code changes needed.

venues.name: Twinsbet Arena
venues.formerName: Avia Solutions Group Arena / Siemens Arena
venues.description: Lithuania's largest arena and the primary venue for major events in the Baltic region. Capacity up to 12,500 for combat sports configurations. A natural host for Baltic Championship editions held in Lithuania.

venues.name: Unibet Arena
venues.description: Estonia's largest indoor arena, located in Tallinn. Seats up to 7,200 in standard configuration with flexible layout options. The designated Baltic Championship host when Estonia holds the rotation.

venues.name: OVO Arena Wembley
venues.description: One of the UK's premier boxing venues. Matchroom Boxing has used it extensively for world title cards. Has hosted Oleksandr Usyk, Anthony Joshua, and multiple world title events. A regular candidate for European Amateur Boxing Championships.

venues.name: WiZink Center
venues.formerName: Palacio de los Deportes de la Comunidad de Madrid
venues.description: Madrid's premier indoor arena seating up to 15,000 for boxing events. Spain has a strong amateur boxing tradition and the WiZink Center has hosted European-level combat sports events.

venues.name: Accor Arena
venues.formerName: Palais Omnisports de Paris-Bercy / AccorHotels Arena
venues.description: Paris's largest indoor arena with a boxing configuration capacity around 16,000. France's strong amateur boxing heritage makes this a natural host for EUBC championships.

venues.name: Belgrade Arena
venues.description: The largest arena in the Western Balkans with a total seated capacity of 18,386. Has hosted European Boxing Championships. Serbia has a strong tradition in amateur combat sports and the arena is a frequent host for continental and world-level championships.

venues.name: Hallenstadion
venues.description: Zurich's Hallenstadion, seating around 11,200 for standard events. A respected European multi-sport venue used for boxing along with tennis, concerts, and team sports. Its central European location makes it a neutral-ground option for EUBC events.

venues.name: Ziggo Dome
venues.description: Amsterdam's premier large-scale indoor venue seating up to 17,000. The Netherlands has hosted European-level boxing events and the Ziggo Dome provides one of the largest European boxing venues outside the UK.

venues.name: MGM Grand Garden Arena
venues.description: The spiritual home of world championship boxing, seating approximately 17,000. Has hosted Floyd Mayweather Jr. vs Manny Pacquiao and fights featuring Mike Tyson, Evander Holyfield, and Oscar De La Hoya. An iconic venue synonymous with big-fight atmosphere.

venues.name: Madison Square Garden
venues.description: The most storied boxing arena in history, seating approximately 20,000. Has hosted Muhammad Ali, Joe Frazier, Rocky Marciano, and virtually every great fighter of the twentieth century. The 'Mecca of Boxing'.

venues.name: Ryōgoku Kokugikan
venues.description: Japan's premier combat sports arena, primarily home to sumo wrestling but converted for Olympic boxing at the 2020 Tokyo Games (held 2021). Its history as an Olympic boxing venue and unique cultural identity make it one of the most distinctive venues in international boxing.

venues.name: Crypto.com Arena
venues.description: Los Angeles's premier sports and entertainment arena, seating up to 19,395 for boxing events. Scheduled to host boxing finals at the 2028 Summer Olympics. Has hosted major world title boxing nights alongside its role as home of the Lakers and Kings.

venues.name: ExCeL London
venues.description: Exhibition and convention centre in east London converted into a boxing arena for the 2012 Summer Olympics, hosting all Olympic boxing competition from 28 July to 12 August. One of the few venues with direct Olympic boxing pedigree in Europe.
